title: Smoky Indian Lamb Kidney Stir-fry
description: A smoky and juicy Indian dish featuring lamb kidneys that is sugar-free-friendly.

ingredients.name: 1 cup fresh cilantro
ingredients.notes: Fresh leaves, stems can be used

ingredients.name: 300g lamb kidneys
ingredients.notes: Rinse and pat dry with paper towels

ingredients.name: 2 tomatoes, finely chopped
ingredients.notes: Can be added as a side to balance flavors or pureed into sauce for stir-fry

ingredients.name: 2 cups water or vegetable broth
ingredients.notes: Use for braising and also to make a sauce

ingredients.name: Salt to taste
ingredients.notes: For seasoning the kidneys and final taste adjustment

instructions: Rinse the lamb kidneys under cold water, pat dry with paper towels. Season them lightly with salt.
Heat a little oil in a pan over medium heat. Brown the kidneys on all sides until golden brown and slightly crisp. Set aside.
In the same pan or a separate one, add more oil if needed. Add onion, ginger, garlic, and cook for about 2 minutes.
Add tomatoes and garam masala powder to the mixture. Stir well and let it cook until the tomato pieces soften slightly.
Bring water or broth to boil in a pot, then add kidneys with their cooking liquid (if any). Let it simmer for 10-15 minutes or until tender but not mushy.
In a bowl, mix the ground cumin seeds and turmeric powder. Drizzle this mixture over the cooked kidney mixture gently while stirring.
Return to the pan where the onions are, add more oil if necessary, then toss in the chopped cilantro. Cook for about 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the stewed kidneys with their juices back into the pan and give a good stir. Adjust seasoning with salt if needed.
If using as a side to vegetables or pureed, you can blend some tomatoes into sauce; otherwise serve kidneys hot in the pan over the vegetables.
